indicator_text: lobbying means promoting specific interests by communication with a public official as part of a structured and organised action aimed at influencing public decision making.
source_quote: a. \lobbying\" means promoting specific interests by communication with a public official as part of a structured and organised action aimed at influencing public decision making (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:161-163)"
notes: Definitional, not a regulatory item; sets scope.

indicator_text: lobbyist means any natural or legal person who engages in lobbying.
source_quote: b. \lobbyist\" means any natural or legal person who engages in lobbying (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:165)"
notes: Definitional; covers paid and unpaid, private and public-interest.

indicator_text: public decision making means decision making within the legislative and executive branches, whether at national, regional or local level.
source_quote: c. \public decision making\" means decision making within the legislative and executive branches, whether at national, regional or local level (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:167-168)"
notes: Definitional; multi-level scope.

indicator_text: public official means any person exercising a public function, whether elected, employed or otherwise, in the legislative or executive branches.
source_quote: d. \public official\" means any person exercising a public function, whether elected, employed or otherwise, in the legislative or executive branches (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:170-171)"
notes: Definitional; broad coverage including advisors per para 19.

indicator_text: legal regulation means statutory regulation, a system of self-regulation or a combination of both.
source_quote: e. \legal regulation\" means statutory regulation, a system of self-regulation or a combination of both (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:173-174)"
notes: Definitional; permits hybrid schemes.

indicator_text: Legal regulation of lobbying should promote the transparency of lobbying activities.
source_quote: 1. Legal regulation of lobbying should promote the transparency of lobbying activities. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:178-179)
notes: The overarching objective principle.

indicator_text: Consultant lobbyists acting on behalf of a third party should be subject to legal regulation.
section_or_category: B. Activities subject to legal regulation
source_quote: Lobbying activities in at least the following categories should be subject to legal regulation: a. consultant lobbyists acting on behalf of a third party (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:184-186)
notes: At least wording — minimum coverage standard.

indicator_text: In-house lobbyists acting on behalf of their employer should be subject to legal regulation.
section_or_category: B. Activities subject to legal regulation
source_quote: b. in-house lobbyists acting on behalf of their employer (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:187)

indicator_text: Organisations or bodies representing professional or other sectoral interests should be subject to legal regulation.
section_or_category: B. Activities subject to legal regulation
source_quote: c. organisations or bodies representing professional or other sectoral interests (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:188-189)
notes: Includes trade unions, employers' orgs, NGOs, civil society per para 26.c.

indicator_text: Exemptions to legal regulations on lobbying should be clearly defined and justified.
section_or_category: B. Activities subject to legal regulation
source_quote: 3. Exemptions to legal regulations on lobbying should be clearly defined and justified. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:190-191)
notes: Constraint on scope-narrowing exemptions.

indicator_text: Legal regulation of lobbying should not infringe the right of individuals to express their opinions and petition public officials, bodies and institutions, whether individually or collectively.
section_or_category: C. Freedom of expression, political activities and participation in public life
source_quote: a. express their opinions and petition public officials, bodies and institutions, whether individually or collectively (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:197-198)
notes: Rights-protective constraint on regulatory design.

indicator_text: Legal regulation of lobbying should not infringe the right of individuals to campaign for political change and change in legislation, policy or practice within the framework of legitimate political activities, individually or collectively.
section_or_category: C. Freedom of expression, political activities and participation in public life
source_quote: b. campaign for political change and change in legislation, policy or practice within the framework of legitimate political activities, individually or collectively (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:199-201)

indicator_text: Information on lobbying activities in the context of public decision-making processes should be disclosed.
source_quote: 5. Information on lobbying activities in the context of public decision-making processes should be disclosed. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:204-205)
notes: Mode of disclosure left to member State (legislative footprint, open diaries, register, etc.).

indicator_text: The rules on disclosure should be proportionate to the importance of the subject matter of the public decision-making process and should reflect constitutional guarantees.
source_quote: 6. The rules on disclosure should be proportionate to the importance of the subject matter of the public decision-making process and should reflect constitutional guarantees. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:206-208)
notes: Proportionality principle; allows lighter touch for low-impact decisions.

indicator_text: A register of lobbyists should be maintained by public authorities or other designated bodies.
source_quote: 7. A register of lobbyists should be maintained by public authorities or other designated bodies. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:211-212)
notes: Plural permits federal/devolved or self-regulatory delegated registers.

indicator_text: Information held in the register should be of a declaratory character. Lobbyists should be responsible for ensuring the information is accurate and up to date.
source_quote: 8. Information held in the register should be of a declaratory character. Lobbyists should be responsible for ensuring the information is accurate and up to date. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:213-215)
notes: Declaratory model with lobbyist self-attestation duty.

indicator_text: The register should be easily accessible and user-friendly. It should be available online with easy-to-use search facilities, open to the public, and consultation should be free of charge.
source_quote: 9. The register should be easily accessible and user-friendly. It should be available online with easy-to-use search facilities, open to the public, and consultation should be free of charge. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:219-221)
notes: Specifies four concrete attributes: online, searchable, public, free.

indicator_text: The processing of personal data from the register should comply with applicable standards on personal data protection.
source_quote: 10. The processing of personal data from the register should comply with applicable standards on personal data protection. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:223-224)
notes: References ETS No. 108 and EU instruments per para 39.

indicator_text: The register should include as a minimum the name and contact details of the lobbyist.
source_quote: Information held in the register should include as a minimum: a. the name and contact details of the lobbyist (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:226-227)
notes: Para 40 elaborates: name, address/phone/website, corporate designation, legal form, registration number, fields of activity.

indicator_text: The register should include as a minimum the subject matter of the lobbying activities.
source_quote: b. the subject matter of the lobbying activities (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:228)
notes: Para 40: targeted policy/legislation areas, interests represented, public department contacted.

indicator_text: The register should include as a minimum the identity of the client or employer, where applicable.
source_quote: c. the identity of the client or employer, where applicable (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:229)
notes: Where applicable — independent consultants must list orgs they work for per para 40.

indicator_text: In order to further promote transparency, registers may include additional information in accordance with national conditions and requirements.
source_quote: 12. In order to further promote transparency, registers may include additional information in accordance with national conditions and requirements. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:231-232)
notes: Permissive — para 42-43 lists examples (number of lobbyists, former officials, financial info).

indicator_text: Where a member State can demonstrate that alternative mechanisms guarantee public access to information on lobbying activities and ensure equivalent levels of accessibility and transparency, it may be considered that the requirement for a public register is satisfied.
source_quote: 13. In the case where a member State can demonstrate that alternative mechanisms guarantee public access to information on lobbying activities and ensure equivalent levels of accessibility and transparency, it may be considered that the requirement for a public register is satisfied. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:234-237)
notes: Open-government carve-out (Nordic model); register replaceable by equivalent transparency.

indicator_text: Lobbyists should be guided by the principles of openness, transparency, honesty and integrity.
section_or_category: F. Standards of ethical behaviour for lobbyists
source_quote: 14. Lobbyists should be guided by the principles of openness, transparency, honesty and integrity. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:241-242)
notes: Chapeau ethical principles; mirrors EU Commission COM(2008)323 per para 45.

indicator_text: Lobbyists should be expected to provide accurate and correct information on their lobbying assignment to the public official concerned.
section_or_category: F. Standards of ethical behaviour for lobbyists
source_quote: a. provide accurate and correct information on their lobbying assignment to the public official concerned (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:243-244)

indicator_text: Lobbyists should be expected to act honestly and in good faith in relation to the lobbying assignment and in all contact with public officials.
section_or_category: F. Standards of ethical behaviour for lobbyists
source_quote: b. act honestly and in good faith in relation to the lobbying assignment and in all contact with public officials (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:245-246)

indicator_text: Lobbyists should be expected to refrain from undue and improper influence over public officials and the public decision-making process.
section_or_category: F. Standards of ethical behaviour for lobbyists
source_quote: c. refrain from undue and improper influence over public officials and the public decision-making process (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:247-248)

indicator_text: Lobbyists should be expected to avoid conflicts of interest.
section_or_category: F. Standards of ethical behaviour for lobbyists
source_quote: d. avoid conflicts of interest (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:249)
notes: Per para 45: covers COI both with public officials and with client/employer.

indicator_text: Legal regulations on lobbying should contain sanctions for non-compliance. These sanctions should be effective, proportionate and dissuasive.
source_quote: 15. Legal regulations on lobbying should contain sanctions for non-compliance. These sanctions should be effective, proportionate and dissuasive. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:253-254)
notes: Triadic sanction standard (effective/proportionate/dissuasive) is concrete; types per para 47: fines, removal, imprisonment.

indicator_text: Appropriate measures tailored to national circumstances should be in place in order to avoid risks to public sector integrity that might be created by lobbying activities.
source_quote: 16. Appropriate measures tailored to national circumstances should be in place in order to avoid risks to public sector integrity that might be created by lobbying activities. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:259-261)
notes: General mandate; P17 lists specific options.

indicator_text: Measures could include a "cooling-off" period before either a public official may become a lobbyist after leaving public employment or office, or a lobbyist may become a public official after ceasing lobbying activities.
source_quote: a. a \cooling-off\" period, namely a period of time that has to elapse before either a public official may become a lobbyist after leaving public employment or office, or a lobbyist may become a public official after ceasing lobbying activities (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:263-266)"
notes: Bidirectional revolving door. Para 53: examples 12-18 mo Germany, 1 yr Lithuania, 5 yr Canada federal.

indicator_text: Measures could include guidance to public officials concerning refusing or disclosing the receipt of gifts and hospitality offered by a lobbyist.
source_quote: - refusing or disclosing the receipt of gifts and hospitality offered by a lobbyist (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:269-270)
notes: Sub-bullet of 17.b guidance.

indicator_text: Measures could include guidance to public officials concerning how to respond to communications from lobbyists.
source_quote: - how to respond to communications from lobbyists (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:271)
notes: Sub-bullet of 17.b guidance.

indicator_text: Measures could include guidance to public officials concerning reporting violations of the regulations or rules of conduct on lobbying activities.
source_quote: - reporting violations of the regulations or rules of conduct on lobbying activities (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:272-273)
notes: Sub-bullet of 17.b guidance.

indicator_text: Measures could include guidance to public officials concerning disclosing conflicts of interest.
source_quote: - disclosing conflicts of interest (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:274)
notes: Sub-bullet of 17.b guidance.

indicator_text: Measures could include guidance to public officials concerning preserving the confidentiality of data.
source_quote: - preserving the confidentiality of data (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:275)
notes: Sub-bullet of 17.b guidance.

indicator_text: Oversight of the regulations on lobbying activities should be entrusted to designated public authorities.
source_quote: 18. Oversight of the regulations on lobbying activities should be entrusted to designated public authorities. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:278-279)
notes: Para 55: existing authorities may be repurposed; need not be new body.

indicator_text: Oversight may include monitoring compliance with the regulations.
source_quote: a. monitoring compliance with the regulations (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:281)
notes: Verifying registration accuracy per para 56.

indicator_text: Oversight may include providing guidance to lobbyists and public officials on the application of the regulations.
source_quote: b. providing guidance to lobbyists and public officials on the application of the regulations (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:282-283)

indicator_text: Oversight may include raising awareness among lobbyists, public officials and the public.
source_quote: c. raising awareness among lobbyists, public officials and the public (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:284)
notes: Para 56: includes media campaigns.

indicator_text: The framework for the legal regulation of lobbying activities should be kept under review.
source_quote: 20. The framework for the legal regulation of lobbying activities should be kept under review. (papers/text/CouncilEurope_2017__lobbying_legal_regulation.txt:287-288)
notes: Para 58: periodicity and reviewing body left to member State.
